```{r}
# ── Part B: CONTEMPORANEOUS governance PCA — for robustness ──

gov_vars_contemp <- c("reg_quality", "corruption", "pol_stability", "voice_acc")

gov_contemp_idx <- complete.cases(df_world_2000_2019[, gov_vars_contemp])

pca_contemp <- prcomp(df_world_2000_2019[gov_contemp_idx, gov_vars_contemp], scale. = TRUE)

cat("\n\n══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("  GOVERNANCE PCA — CONTEMPORANEOUS\n")
cat("──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  Variables:", paste(gov_vars_contemp, collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("  Complete cases:", sum(gov_contemp_idx), "of", nrow(df_world_2000_2019), "\n")
cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("── Variance explained ──\n")
print(round(summary(pca_contemp)$importance, 3))

cat("\n── PC1 loadings ──\n")
print(round(pca_contemp$rotation[, 1], 3))

# Attach contemporaneous PC1 scores
df_world_2000_2019$gov_pca <- NA_real_
df_world_2000_2019$gov_pca[gov_contemp_idx] <- pca_contemp$x[, 1]

cat(sprintf("\n── gov_pca created: %d non-missing values ──\n",
            sum(!is.na(df_world_2000_2019$gov_pca))))
print(summary(df_world_2000_2019$gov_pca))

# Update df_est with both PCA variables
df_est <- df_world_2000_2019 %>%
  filter(country %in% keep_countries)

# Clean up
rm(gov_vars_lag, gov_vars_contemp, gov_lag_idx, gov_contemp_idx,
   pca_lag, pca_contemp)

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# INTERPRETATION OF RESULTS
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Both versions produce virtually identical results — expected since
# governance variables are 91–97% between-country (Diagnostic 3) and
# lagging by one year barely changes them.
#
# LAGGED (t-1):
#   PC1 explains 84.3% of variance. Loadings: reg_quality (0.508),
#   corruption (0.518), pol_stability (0.472), voice_acc (0.500).
#   3,443 non-missing values. Range: -4.52 to +4.03.

# CONTEMPORANEOUS:
#   PC1 explains 84.3% of variance. Loadings: identical to lagged version.
#   3,637 non-missing values (194 more — gains one year per country since
#   no lag is consumed). Range: -4.53 to +4.03.

# Key observations:
#   - Loadings are nearly equal across all four variables. No single
#     indicator dominates — PC1 is a balanced "institutional quality" index.
#   - pol_stability has the lowest loading (0.472), consistent with it
#     capturing something modestly distinct (security/conflict risk vs.
#     bureaucratic/regulatory quality). This supports using it as the
#     standalone baseline control — it's the least redundant of the four.
#   - 84.3% variance explained is very strong. The four governance
#     indicators are effectively measuring one latent construct.

# Usage in the LP specification:
#   - Baseline: pol_stability_t_minus1 (single governance control)
#   - Robustness A: swap for reg_quality_t_minus1, corruption_t_minus1, or
#     voice_acc_t_minus1 (one at a time)
#   - Robustness B: swap for gov_pca_t_minus1 (lagged composite)
#   - Robustness C: swap for gov_pca (contemporaneous composite, tests
#     whether the pre-determination lag matters for governance controls)

# The near-identical results between lagged and contemporaneous PCA confirm
# that governance is overwhelmingly a cross-country characteristic — the
# lag is a formality for econometric cleanliness, not a substantive choice.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

```

```{r}
#| label: Governance PCA Visualized — Scree Plot
#| output: true
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5

# Scree plot showing variance explained by each principal component.
# The sharp drop after PC1 (84.3%) confirms a single component is sufficient.

# Re-run PCA briefly to extract variance shares for plotting
gov_vars_pca <- c("reg_quality_t_minus1", "corruption_t_minus1",
                  "pol_stability_t_minus1", "voice_acc_t_minus1")
pca_fit <- prcomp(df_world_2000_2019[complete.cases(df_world_2000_2019[, gov_vars_pca]), gov_vars_pca],
                  scale. = TRUE)

# Build plotting data
scree_df <- data.frame(
  PC = 1:length(pca_fit$sdev),
  Variance_Pct = (pca_fit$sdev^2 / sum(pca_fit$sdev^2)) * 100
)

ggplot(scree_df, aes(x = PC, y = Variance_Pct)) +
  geom_line(color = "#2C7FB8", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, color = "#2C7FB8", fill = "white", size = 3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 5, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  annotate("text", x = 3.7, y = 7,
           label = "5% threshold", color = "grey40", size = 3.5, family = "serif") +
  annotate("text", x = 1.3, y = scree_df$Variance_Pct[1] - 3,
           label = paste0("PC1: ", round(scree_df$Variance_Pct[1], 1), "%"),
           color = "#2C7FB8", fontface = "bold", size = 4, family = "serif") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:nrow(scree_df)) +
  labs(title = "Governance PCA — Scree Plot",
       subtitle = "PC1 alone explains 84.3% of variance across four governance indicators",
       x = "Principal Component",
       y = "% Variance Explained") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "serif") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

# Clean up
rm(gov_vars_pca, pca_fit, scree_df)

```


```{r}
#| label: Final Environment Setup for LP Estimation
#| output: true

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FINAL ENVIRONMENT SETUP — PREPARE FOR LP ESTIMATION
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#
# PURPOSE:
# Ensure df_est is the single canonical dataset going forward, define all
# regressor and specification vectors for the LP models, and clean the
# environment. After this chunk, only estimation-relevant objects remain.
#
# LP ESTIMATION PLAN:
# We estimate local projections for 2 main + 1 sideline dependent variables:
#
#   Part A — Inflation response (MAIN):
#     A1. Full sample, A2. Developed only, A3. Emerging only
#
#   Part B — Log GDP per capita response (MAIN):
#     B1. Full sample, B2. Developed only, B3. Emerging only
#
#   Part C — GDP growth response (SIDELINE, supplementary):
#     C1. Full sample, C2. Developed only, C3. Emerging only
#
# Each set is estimated for horizons h = 0, 1, ..., 5 using the baseline
# specification + alternative governance robustness checks.
#
# GOVERNANCE CONTROLS:
# Governance variables enter contemporaneously (not lagged). Justification:
#   - Bounded indices [-2.5, 2.5] — unit root logically impossible
#   - 91–97% between-country variation (Diagnostic 3) — contemporaneous
#     and lagged values are virtually identical
#   - Gains ~194 extra observations vs. lagged version
#   - PCA confirmed identical results for lagged vs. contemporaneous
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ── 1. Rebuild df_est one final time to capture all columns ──
df_est <- df_world_2000_2019 %>%
  filter(country %in% keep_countries)

# ── 2. Define regressor vectors ──

# Baseline specification (contemporaneous governance: pol_stability)
baseline_regressors <- c("mps_w", "mimic_t_minus1", "mps_w_x_mimic",
                         "gdp_growth_w_t_minus1", "infl_w_t_minus1",
                         "credit_gdp_t_minus1", "gov_pca")

# Alternative governance specifications (swap pol_stability for one of these)
alt_gov_regressors <- list(
  reg_quality = c("mps_w", "mimic_t_minus1", "mps_w_x_mimic",
                  "gdp_growth_w_t_minus1", "infl_w_t_minus1",
                  "credit_gdp_t_minus1", "reg_quality"),
  corruption  = c("mps_w", "mimic_t_minus1", "mps_w_x_mimic",
                  "gdp_growth_w_t_minus1", "infl_w_t_minus1",
                  "credit_gdp_t_minus1", "corruption"),
  voice_acc   = c("mps_w", "mimic_t_minus1", "mps_w_x_mimic",
                  "gdp_growth_w_t_minus1", "infl_w_t_minus1",
                  "credit_gdp_t_minus1", "voice_acc"),
  pol_stab    = c("mps_w", "mimic_t_minus1", "mps_w_x_mimic",
                  "gdp_growth_w_t_minus1", "infl_w_t_minus1",
                  "credit_gdp_t_minus1", "pol_stability")
)

# ── 3. Define dependent variable and sample configurations ──

# Dependent variables by horizon
dep_vars <- list(
  inflation  = paste0("d_infl_w_h", 0:max_horizon),     # Main dep 1 (winsorized)
  lgdp_pc    = paste0("d_lgdp_pc_h", 0:max_horizon),    # Main dep 2 (raw, log-transformed)
  gdp_growth = paste0("d_gdp_growth_w_h", 0:max_horizon) # Sideline (winsorized)
)

# Sample splits
samples <- list(
  full      = NULL,                     # No filter — all countries
  developed = "Developed",              # dev_status == "Developed"
  emerging  = "Emerging"                # dev_status == "Emerging"
)

# ── 4. Remove everything except what LP estimation needs ──
rm(keep_countries)

remaining <- setdiff(ls(), c("df_est", "baseline_regressors",
                              "alt_gov_regressors", "dep_vars",
                              "samples", "max_horizon"))
if (length(remaining) > 0) rm(list = remaining)
rm(remaining)

# ── 5. Report final state ──
cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("  ENVIRONMENT READY FOR LP ESTIMATION\n")
cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("── Objects in environment ──\n")
for (obj in ls()) {
  cat(sprintf("  %-25s %s\n", obj, class(get(obj))[1]))
}

cat("\n── Dataset ──\n")
cat(sprintf("  df_est: %d rows × %d cols | %d countries | %d years\n",
            nrow(df_est), ncol(df_est),
            dplyr::n_distinct(df_est$country),
            dplyr::n_distinct(df_est$year)))

cat(sprintf("\n── Horizons: 0 to %d ──\n", max_horizon))

cat("\n── Baseline regressors ──\n")
cat(sprintf("  %s\n", paste(baseline_regressors, collapse = ", ")))

cat("\n── Alternative governance specs ──\n")
for (name in names(alt_gov_regressors)) {
  gov_var <- tail(alt_gov_regressors[[name]], 1)
  cat(sprintf("  %-15s → swap governance PCA for %s\n", name, gov_var))
}

cat("\n── Dependent variables ──\n")
for (name in names(dep_vars)) {
  cat(sprintf("  %-15s → %s ... %s\n", name,
              dep_vars[[name]][1], tail(dep_vars[[name]], 1)))
}

cat("\n── Sample splits ──\n")
for (name in names(samples)) {
  if (is.null(samples[[name]])) {
    n <- dplyr::n_distinct(df_est$country)
    cat(sprintf("  %-15s → all countries (%d)\n", name, n))
  } else {
    n <- dplyr::n_distinct(df_est$country[df_est$dev_status == samples[[name]]])
    cat(sprintf("  %-15s → %s (%d countries)\n", name, samples[[name]], n))
  }
}

cat("\n── LP estimation plan: 9 core sets (6 main + 3 sideline) ──\n")
cat("  A1. Inflation     × Full sample\n")
cat("  A2. Inflation     × Developed only\n")
cat("  A3. Inflation     × Emerging only\n")
cat("  B1. Log GDP p.c.  × Full sample\n")
cat("  B2. Log GDP p.c.  × Developed only\n")
cat("  B3. Log GDP p.c.  × Emerging only\n")
cat("  C1. GDP growth    × Full sample     (sideline)\n")
cat("  C2. GDP growth    × Developed only  (sideline)\n")
cat("  C3. GDP growth    × Emerging only   (sideline)\n")
cat("  + 4 alternative governance specs for main deps (robustness)\n")

cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")

```


```{r}
#| label: LP Estimation — Helper Function
#| output: false

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# LOCAL PROJECTIONS — HELPER FUNCTION
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#
# PURPOSE:
# This function estimates the panel local projections model (Jordà 2005)
# for a given dependent variable prefix, sample, and set of regressors
# across all horizons h = 0, ..., max_horizon.
#
# MODEL (for each horizon h):
#   d_Y_h = α_i + λ_t + β·MPS + γ·MIMIC + δ·(MPS×MIMIC) + θ'X + ε
#
# where:
#   α_i = country fixed effects (absorb time-invariant country characteristics)
#   λ_t = year fixed effects (absorb global shocks common to all countries)
#   δ   = coefficient of interest — how informality moderates monetary transmission
#   SEs clustered at the country level (accounts for serial correlation within countries)
#
# RETURNS:
# A tidy dataframe with one row per horizon, containing the coefficient
# estimates, standard errors, confidence intervals, and sample sizes for
# all regressors — designed for easy IRF plotting.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

run_lp <- function(data, dep_prefix, regressors, horizons = 0:max_horizon,
                   spec_label = "baseline", sample_label = "full") {

  results <- list()

  for (h in horizons) {
    dep_var <- paste0(dep_prefix, h)

    # Skip if dependent variable doesn't exist
    if (!dep_var %in% names(data)) {
      warning(paste("Variable", dep_var, "not found — skipping horizon", h))
      next
    }

    # Build formula: dep_var ~ regressors | country + year
    fml <- as.formula(paste(dep_var, "~",
                            paste(regressors, collapse = " + "),
                            "| country + year"))

    # Estimate with country-clustered SEs
    fit <- tryCatch({
      feols(fml, data = data, cluster = "country")
    }, error = function(e) {
      warning(paste("Horizon", h, "failed:", conditionMessage(e)))
      return(NULL)
    })

    if (is.null(fit)) next

    # Extract coefficients into tidy format
    coef_table <- as.data.frame(coeftable(fit))
    coef_table$variable <- rownames(coef_table)
    rownames(coef_table) <- NULL

    coef_table <- coef_table %>%
      rename(
        estimate = Estimate,
        std_error = `Std. Error`,
        t_value = `t value`,
        p_value = `Pr(>|t|)`
      ) %>%
      mutate(
        horizon      = h,
        ci_lower_90  = estimate - 1.645 * std_error,
        ci_upper_90  = estimate + 1.645 * std_error,
        ci_lower_95  = estimate - 1.96 * std_error,
        ci_upper_95  = estimate + 1.96 * std_error,
        n_obs        = fit$nobs,
        n_countries  = length(unique(fit$fixef_sizes)),
        spec         = spec_label,
        sample       = sample_label,
        dep_var_type = dep_prefix
      )

    results[[as.character(h)]] <- coef_table
  }

  bind_rows(results)
}

```


```{r}
#| label: Quick LP test
#| output: true

# Quick test: one regression at h = 0 to verify feols works
test_fml <- d_infl_w_h0 ~ mps_w + mimic_t_minus1 + mps_w_x_mimic +
            gdp_growth_w_t_minus1 + credit_gdp_t_minus1 + gov_pca | country + year

test_fit <- feols(test_fml, data = df_est, cluster = "country")
summary(test_fit)

rm(test_fml, test_fit)

# Works!

```

```{r}
#| label: LP Estimation — Part A Inflation Response
#| output: true
#| warning: false

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PART A — INFLATION RESPONSE TO MONETARY POLICY SHOCKS
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#
# Dependent variable: d_infl_w_h{h} = π_{i,t+h} − π_{i,t−1}
#   (cumulative change in winsorized inflation from pre-shock baseline)
#
# Baseline specification:
#   d_infl_w_h = mps_w + mimic_t_minus1 + mps_w_x_mimic
#              + gdp_growth_w_t_minus1 + credit_gdp_t_minus1 + gov_pca
#              | country + year
#
# Note: lagged inflation is NOT included as a control because the
# dependent variable is already defined relative to infl_{t-1}.
#
# Estimated for 3 samples: Full, Developed, Emerging
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Define inflation-specific regressors
infl_regressors <- c("mps_w", "mimic_t_minus1", "mps_w_x_mimic",
                     "gdp_growth_w_t_minus1", "credit_gdp_t_minus1", "gov_pca")

# ── A1: Full sample ──
cat("Running Part A1: Inflation × Full sample...\n")
lp_infl_full <- run_lp(df_est, "d_infl_w_h", infl_regressors,
                        spec_label = "baseline", sample_label = "full")

# ── A2: Developed only ──
cat("Running Part A2: Inflation × Developed...\n")
lp_infl_dev <- run_lp(df_est %>% filter(dev_status == "Developed"),
                       "d_infl_w_h", infl_regressors,
                       spec_label = "baseline", sample_label = "developed")

# ── A3: Emerging only ──
cat("Running Part A3: Inflation × Emerging...\n")
lp_infl_emg <- run_lp(df_est %>% filter(dev_status == "Emerging"),
                       "d_infl_w_h", infl_regressors,
                       spec_label = "baseline", sample_label = "emerging")

# Combine all inflation results
lp_inflation <- bind_rows(lp_infl_full, lp_infl_dev, lp_infl_emg)
rm(lp_infl_full, lp_infl_dev, lp_infl_emg)

# ── Report key coefficient: δ_h (interaction MPS × MIMIC) ──
cat("\n══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("  PART A — INFLATION: MPS × MIMIC INTERACTION (δ_h)\n")
cat("──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  δ > 0 → informality dampens inflation response to monetary shock\n")
cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

interaction_infl <- lp_inflation %>%
  filter(variable == "mps_w_x_mimic") %>%
  select(sample, horizon, estimate, std_error, p_value, ci_lower_95, ci_upper_95, n_obs)

interaction_infl$estimate     <- round(interaction_infl$estimate, 4)
interaction_infl$std_error    <- round(interaction_infl$std_error, 4)
interaction_infl$p_value      <- round(interaction_infl$p_value, 3)
interaction_infl$ci_lower_95  <- round(interaction_infl$ci_lower_95, 4)
interaction_infl$ci_upper_95  <- round(interaction_infl$ci_upper_95, 4)

print(as.data.frame(interaction_infl), row.names = FALSE)

```


```{r}
#| label: LP Estimation — Part B Log GDP per Capita Response
#| output: true
#| warning: false

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PART B — LOG GDP PER CAPITA RESPONSE TO MONETARY POLICY SHOCKS (MAIN)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#
# Dependent variable: d_lgdp_pc_h{h} = log(gdp_pc_{i,t+h}) − log(gdp_pc_{i,t−1})
#   (cumulative log-change in GDP per capita from pre-shock baseline)
#
# Baseline specification:
#   d_lgdp_pc_h = mps_w + mimic_t_minus1 + mps_w_x_mimic
#               + infl_w_t_minus1 + credit_gdp_t_minus1 + gov_pca
#               | country + year
#
# Note: log(gdp_pc) is NOT winsorized — the log transformation already
# dampens extreme values. Lagged inflation is included as a cross-variable
# control. GDP growth is NOT included (collinear with log GDP changes).
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Define log GDP p.c. specific regressors
lgdp_pc_regressors <- c("mps_w", "mimic_t_minus1", "mps_w_x_mimic",
                         "infl_w_t_minus1", "credit_gdp_t_minus1", "gov_pca")

# ── B1: Full sample ──
cat("Running Part B1: Log GDP p.c. × Full sample...\n")
lp_lgdp_full <- run_lp(df_est, "d_lgdp_pc_h", lgdp_pc_regressors,
                        spec_label = "baseline", sample_label = "full")

# ── B2: Developed only ──
cat("Running Part B2: Log GDP p.c. × Developed...\n")
lp_lgdp_dev <- run_lp(df_est %>% filter(dev_status == "Developed"),
                       "d_lgdp_pc_h", lgdp_pc_regressors,
                       spec_label = "baseline", sample_label = "developed")

# ── B3: Emerging only ──
cat("Running Part B3: Log GDP p.c. × Emerging...\n")
lp_lgdp_emg <- run_lp(df_est %>% filter(dev_status == "Emerging"),
                       "d_lgdp_pc_h", lgdp_pc_regressors,
                       spec_label = "baseline", sample_label = "emerging")

# Combine all log GDP p.c. results
lp_lgdp_pc <- bind_rows(lp_lgdp_full, lp_lgdp_dev, lp_lgdp_emg)
rm(lp_lgdp_full, lp_lgdp_dev, lp_lgdp_emg)

# ── Report key coefficient: δ_h (interaction MPS × MIMIC) ──
cat("\n══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("  PART B — LOG GDP PER CAPITA: MPS × MIMIC INTERACTION (δ_h)\n")
cat("──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  δ > 0 → informality dampens output response to monetary shock\n")
cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

interaction_lgdp <- lp_lgdp_pc %>%
  filter(variable == "mps_w_x_mimic") %>%
  select(sample, horizon, estimate, std_error, p_value, ci_lower_95, ci_upper_95, n_obs)

interaction_lgdp$estimate     <- round(interaction_lgdp$estimate, 4)
interaction_lgdp$std_error    <- round(interaction_lgdp$std_error, 4)
interaction_lgdp$p_value      <- round(interaction_lgdp$p_value, 3)
interaction_lgdp$ci_lower_95  <- round(interaction_lgdp$ci_lower_95, 4)
interaction_lgdp$ci_upper_95  <- round(interaction_lgdp$ci_upper_95, 4)

print(as.data.frame(interaction_lgdp), row.names = FALSE)

```
```{r}
#| label: LP Estimation — Part C GDP Growth Response (Sideline)
#| output: true
#| warning: false

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PART C — GDP GROWTH RESPONSE (SIDELINE / SUPPLEMENTARY)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#
# Dependent variable: d_gdp_growth_w_h{h} = gdp_growth_{i,t+h} − gdp_growth_{i,t−1}
#   (cumulative change in winsorized GDP growth rate)
#
# This is a supplementary analysis — the main output response is captured
# by log GDP per capita in Part B. GDP growth is kept for completeness
# and in case the paper needs to discuss growth rate dynamics separately.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Define GDP growth specific regressors
gdp_growth_regressors <- c("mps_w", "mimic_t_minus1", "mps_w_x_mimic",
                           "infl_w_t_minus1", "credit_gdp_t_minus1", "gov_pca")

# ── C1: Full sample ──
cat("Running Part C1: GDP growth × Full sample (sideline)...\n")
lp_gdpgr_full <- run_lp(df_est, "d_gdp_growth_w_h", gdp_growth_regressors,
                         spec_label = "baseline", sample_label = "full")

# ── C2: Developed only ──
cat("Running Part C2: GDP growth × Developed (sideline)...\n")
lp_gdpgr_dev <- run_lp(df_est %>% filter(dev_status == "Developed"),
                        "d_gdp_growth_w_h", gdp_growth_regressors,
                        spec_label = "baseline", sample_label = "developed")

# ── C3: Emerging only ──
cat("Running Part C3: GDP growth × Emerging (sideline)...\n")
lp_gdpgr_emg <- run_lp(df_est %>% filter(dev_status == "Emerging"),
                        "d_gdp_growth_w_h", gdp_growth_regressors,
                        spec_label = "baseline", sample_label = "emerging")


# Combine all GDP growth results
lp_gdp_growth <- bind_rows(lp_gdpgr_full, lp_gdpgr_dev, lp_gdpgr_emg)
rm(lp_gdpgr_full, lp_gdpgr_dev, lp_gdpgr_emg, gdp_growth_regressors)

cat("\n── GDP growth sideline estimation complete ──\n")


# ── Report key coefficient: δ_h (interaction MPS × MIMIC) ──
cat("\n══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("  PART C — GDP GROWTH RESPONS: MPS × MIMIC INTERACTION (δ_h)\n")
cat("──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  δ > 0 → informality dampens output growth response to monetary shock\n")
cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

interaction_gdp_growth <- lp_gdp_growth %>%
  filter(variable == "mps_w_x_mimic") %>%
  select(sample, horizon, estimate, std_error, p_value, ci_lower_95, ci_upper_95, n_obs)

interaction_gdp_growth$estimate     <- round(interaction_gdp_growth$estimate, 4)
interaction_gdp_growth$std_error    <- round(interaction_gdp_growth$std_error, 4)
interaction_gdp_growth$p_value      <- round(interaction_gdp_growth$p_value, 3)
interaction_gdp_growth$ci_lower_95  <- round(interaction_gdp_growth$ci_lower_95, 4)
interaction_gdp_growth$ci_upper_95  <- round(interaction_gdp_growth$ci_upper_95, 4)

print(as.data.frame(interaction_gdp_growth), row.names = FALSE)

```

```{r}
#| label: LP Estimation — Robustness Alternative Governance Specs
#| output: true
#| warning: false

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ROBUSTNESS — ALTERNATIVE GOVERNANCE SPECIFICATIONS
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#
# The baseline uses gov_pca as the governance control. Here we re-estimate
# the full-sample models swapping gov_pca for each individual governance
# variable to verify that δ_h (MPS × MIMIC) is not sensitive to the choice.
#
# Alternatives: reg_quality, corruption, voice_acc, pol_stability
# Run on full sample only (subsample robustness is secondary).
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Define alternative governance specs for INFLATION
alt_gov_infl <- list(
  reg_quality  = c("mps_w", "mimic_t_minus1", "mps_w_x_mimic",
                   "gdp_growth_w_t_minus1", "credit_gdp_t_minus1", "reg_quality"),
  corruption   = c("mps_w", "mimic_t_minus1", "mps_w_x_mimic",
                   "gdp_growth_w_t_minus1", "credit_gdp_t_minus1", "corruption"),
  voice_acc    = c("mps_w", "mimic_t_minus1", "mps_w_x_mimic",
                   "gdp_growth_w_t_minus1", "credit_gdp_t_minus1", "voice_acc"),
  pol_stability = c("mps_w", "mimic_t_minus1", "mps_w_x_mimic",
                   "gdp_growth_w_t_minus1", "credit_gdp_t_minus1", "pol_stability")
)

# Define alternative governance specs for log GDP p.c.
alt_gov_lgdp <- list(
  reg_quality  = c("mps_w", "mimic_t_minus1", "mps_w_x_mimic",
                   "infl_w_t_minus1", "credit_gdp_t_minus1", "reg_quality"),
  corruption   = c("mps_w", "mimic_t_minus1", "mps_w_x_mimic",
                   "infl_w_t_minus1", "credit_gdp_t_minus1", "corruption"),
  voice_acc    = c("mps_w", "mimic_t_minus1", "mps_w_x_mimic",
                   "infl_w_t_minus1", "credit_gdp_t_minus1", "voice_acc"),
  pol_stability = c("mps_w", "mimic_t_minus1", "mps_w_x_mimic",
                   "infl_w_t_minus1", "credit_gdp_t_minus1", "pol_stability")
)

# Run all alternative governance specs on full sample
lp_robustness <- list()

for (gov_name in names(alt_gov_infl)) {
  cat(sprintf("Running robustness: Inflation × %s...\n", gov_name))
  lp_robustness[[paste0("infl_", gov_name)]] <-
    run_lp(df_est, "d_infl_w_h", alt_gov_infl[[gov_name]],
           spec_label = gov_name, sample_label = "full")
}

for (gov_name in names(alt_gov_lgdp)) {
  cat(sprintf("Running robustness: Log GDP p.c. × %s...\n", gov_name))
  lp_robustness[[paste0("lgdp_", gov_name)]] <-
    run_lp(df_est, "d_lgdp_pc_h", alt_gov_lgdp[[gov_name]],
           spec_label = gov_name, sample_label = "full")
}

lp_robust <- bind_rows(lp_robustness)
rm(lp_robustness)

# ── Compare δ_h across governance specs ──
cat("\n══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("  ROBUSTNESS — MPS × MIMIC (δ_h) ACROSS GOVERNANCE SPECS\n")
cat("──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  Stable δ_h across specs → result is not driven by governance choice\n")
cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")

cat("\n── Inflation models ──\n")
lp_robust %>%
  filter(variable == "mps_w_x_mimic", grepl("^infl_", dep_var_type) | dep_var_type == "d_infl_w_h") %>%
  select(spec, horizon, estimate, p_value) %>%
  mutate(estimate = round(estimate, 4), p_value = round(p_value, 3)) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = horizon, values_from = c(estimate, p_value),
              names_glue = "h{horizon}_{.value}") %>%
  print()

cat("\n── Log GDP per capita models ──\n")
lp_robust %>%
  filter(variable == "mps_w_x_mimic", grepl("^lgdp_", dep_var_type) | dep_var_type == "d_lgdp_pc_h") %>%
  select(spec, horizon, estimate, p_value) %>%
  mutate(estimate = round(estimate, 4), p_value = round(p_value, 3)) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = horizon, values_from = c(estimate, p_value),
              names_glue = "h{horizon}_{.value}") %>%
  print()

# Clean up spec vectors
rm(alt_gov_infl, alt_gov_lgdp, infl_regressors, lgdp_pc_regressors, gov_name)

```


```{r}
#| label: LP Results — Combine All Results
#| output: true

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# COMBINE ALL LP RESULTS INTO A SINGLE MASTER DATAFRAME
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#
# lp_all contains every estimated coefficient from:
#   - 6 baseline models (2 dep vars × 3 samples)
#   - 8 robustness models (2 dep vars × 4 alternative governance specs)
#
# This is the single object used for all subsequent IRF plotting and
# results tables. Filter by variable, sample, spec, and dep_var_type
# to extract what you need.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

lp_all <- bind_rows(
  lp_inflation %>% mutate(dep_var_type = "inflation"),
  lp_lgdp_pc %>% mutate(dep_var_type = "lgdp_pc"),
  lp_gdp_growth %>% mutate(dep_var_type = "gdp_growth"),
  lp_robust
)

cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("  ALL LP RESULTS COMBINED: lp_all\n")
cat("──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat(sprintf("  Total coefficient rows: %d\n", nrow(lp_all)))
cat(sprintf("  Unique specs:           %s\n", paste(unique(lp_all$spec), collapse = ", ")))
cat(sprintf("  Unique samples:         %s\n", paste(unique(lp_all$sample), collapse = ", ")))
cat(sprintf("  Unique dep var types:   %s\n", paste(unique(lp_all$dep_var_type), collapse = ", ")))
cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")

# Clean up intermediate objects — keep only lp_all for plotting
rm(lp_inflation, lp_lgdp_pc, lp_gdp_growth, lp_robust)

```


```{r}
#| label: IRF Plots — PART A Inflation Response (Baseline)
#| output: true
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 5

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# IMPULSE RESPONSE FUNCTIONS — INFLATION
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#
# These plots show the estimated δ_h (coefficient on MPS × MIMIC) across
# horizons h = 0, ..., 5 with 90% and 95% confidence bands.
#
# Interpretation (Jordà 2005):
#   Each point is the coefficient from a SEPARATE regression at horizon h.
#   The sequence of coefficients traces out the impulse response function.
#
#   δ_h > 0 → higher informality DAMPENS the inflation response to MPS
#   δ_h < 0 → higher informality AMPLIFIES the inflation response
#   δ_h ≈ 0 → informality doesn't moderate monetary transmission
#   Bands excluding zero → statistically significant at that confidence level
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Extract the interaction coefficient across horizons for each sample
irf_infl <- lp_all %>%
  filter(variable == "mps_w_x_mimic",
         dep_var_type == "inflation",
         spec == "baseline")

# Set sample as ordered factor for faceting
irf_infl$sample <- factor(irf_infl$sample,
                          levels = c("full", "developed", "emerging"),
                          labels = c("Full Sample", "Developed", "Emerging"))

ggplot(irf_infl, aes(x = horizon, y = estimate)) +
  # 95% CI band (lighter)
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower_95, ymax = ci_upper_95),
              fill = "#2C7FB8", alpha = 0.15) +
  # 90% CI band (darker)
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower_90, ymax = ci_upper_90),
              fill = "#2C7FB8", alpha = 0.25) +
  # Point estimates
  geom_line(color = "#2C7FB8", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(color = "#2C7FB8", size = 2.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(estimate, 3)), nudge_y = 0.02, size = 3.5, family = "serif") +
  # Zero reference line
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  # Facet by sample
  facet_wrap(~ sample, ncol = 3) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:max_horizon) +
  labs(
    title = "Impulse Response: Effect of MPS × Informality on Inflation",
    subtitle = expression(paste(delta[h], " from: ", Delta, pi[t+h],
                                " = ", alpha[i], " + ", lambda[t],
                                " + ", beta, "·MPS + ", gamma, "·MIMIC + ",
                                delta, "·(MPS×MIMIC) + ", theta, "'X + ", epsilon)),
    x = "Horizon (years)",
    y = expression(hat(delta)[h]),
    caption = "Shaded areas: 90% (dark) and 95% (light) confidence intervals. Country-clustered SEs."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "serif") +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30")
  )

```
```{r}
#| label: IRF Plots — PART B Log GDP per Capita Response (Baseline)
#| output: true
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 5

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# IMPULSE RESPONSE FUNCTIONS — GDP GROWTH
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

#   δ_h > 0 → higher informality DAMPENS output (GDP) response to monetary shock
#   δ_h < 0 → higher informality AMPLIFIES output (GDP) response to monetary shock


irf_lgdp <- lp_all %>%
  filter(variable == "mps_w_x_mimic",
         dep_var_type == "lgdp_pc",
         spec == "baseline")

irf_lgdp$sample <- factor(irf_lgdp$sample,
                          levels = c("full", "developed", "emerging"),
                          labels = c("Full Sample", "Developed", "Emerging"))

ggplot(irf_lgdp, aes(x = horizon, y = estimate)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower_95, ymax = ci_upper_95),
              fill = "#D95F02", alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower_90, ymax = ci_upper_90),
              fill = "#D95F02", alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_line(color = "#D95F02", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(color = "#D95F02", size = 2.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(estimate, 5)), nudge_y = 0.002, size = 3.5, family = "serif") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  facet_wrap(~ sample, ncol = 3) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:max_horizon) +
  labs(
    title = "Impulse Response: Effect of MPS × Informality on Log GDP per Capita",
    subtitle = expression(paste(delta[h], " from: ", Delta, "log(gdp_pc)"[t+h],
                                " = ", alpha[i], " + ", lambda[t],
                                " + ", beta, "·MPS + ", gamma, "·MIMIC + ",
                                delta, "·(MPS×MIMIC) + ", theta, "'X + ", epsilon)),
    x = "Horizon (years)",
    y = expression(hat(delta)[h]),
    caption = "Shaded areas: 90% (dark) and 95% (light) confidence intervals. Country-clustered SEs."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "serif") +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30")
  )

```


```{r}
#| label: IRF Plots — PART C GDP Growth Response (Baseline)
#| output: true
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 5

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# IMPULSE RESPONSE FUNCTIONS — GDP GROWTH
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

#   δ_h > 0 → higher informality DAMPENS the output (GDP) growth response to MPS
#   δ_h < 0 → higher informality AMPLIFIES the output (GDP) growth response


irf_gdp_growth <- lp_all %>%
  filter(variable == "mps_w_x_mimic",
         dep_var_type == "gdp_growth",
         spec == "baseline")

irf_gdp_growth$sample <- factor(irf_gdp_growth$sample,
                         levels = c("full", "developed", "emerging"),
                         labels = c("Full Sample", "Developed", "Emerging"))

ggplot(irf_gdp_growth, aes(x = horizon, y = estimate)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower_95, ymax = ci_upper_95),
              fill = "#66A61E", alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower_90, ymax = ci_upper_90),
              fill = "#66A61E", alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_line(color = "#66A61E", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(color = "#66A61E", size = 2.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(estimate, 3)), nudge_y = 0.04, size = 3.5, family = "serif") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  facet_wrap(~ sample, ncol = 3) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:max_horizon) +
  labs(
    title = "Impulse Response: Effect of MPS × Informality on GDP Growth",
    subtitle = expression(paste(delta[h], " from: ", Delta, y[t+h],
                                " = ", alpha[i], " + ", lambda[t],
                                " + ", beta, "·MPS + ", gamma, "·MIMIC + ",
                                delta, "·(MPS×MIMIC) + ", theta, "'X + ", epsilon)),
    x = "Horizon (years)",
    y = expression(hat(delta)[h]),
    caption = "Shaded areas: 90% (dark) and 95% (light) confidence intervals. Country-clustered SEs."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "serif") +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30")
  )

```


```{r}
#| label: IRF Plots — Robustness Comparison (Governance Alternatives)
#| output: true
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 10

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ROBUSTNESS IRF — δ_h ACROSS ALTERNATIVE GOVERNANCE SPECIFICATIONS
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#
# If the interaction coefficient δ_h is stable across different governance
# controls, the result is robust to the "which governance variable?" choice.
# Each line is a different governance specification; all on full sample.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Combine baseline (full sample) with robustness specs
irf_robust <- bind_rows(
  lp_all %>%
    filter(variable == "mps_w_x_mimic", spec == "baseline", sample == "full") %>%
    mutate(gov_spec = "gov_pca (baseline)"),
  lp_all %>%
    filter(variable == "mps_w_x_mimic", spec != "baseline", sample == "full") %>%
    mutate(gov_spec = spec)
)

# Separate by dep var type — main dependents only
irf_robust <- irf_robust %>%
  filter(dep_var_type %in% c("inflation", "lgdp_pc", "d_infl_w_h", "d_lgdp_pc_h")) %>%
  mutate(dep_label = case_when(
    grepl("infl", dep_var_type) ~ "Inflation",
    grepl("lgdp", dep_var_type) ~ "Log GDP per Capita",
    TRUE ~ dep_var_type
  ))

ggplot(irf_robust, aes(x = horizon, y = estimate, color = gov_spec)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  facet_wrap(~ dep_label, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:max_horizon) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("gov_pca (baseline)" = "#2C7FB8",
               "reg_quality" = "#E7298A",
               "corruption" = "#66A61E",
               "voice_acc" = "#E6AB02",
               "pol_stability" = "#7570B3"),
    name = "Governance control"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = expression(paste("Robustness: ", delta[h], " (MPS × MIMIC) Across Governance Specifications")),
    subtitle = "Full sample only. Stable trajectories -> result not driven by governance choice.",
    x = "Horizon (years)",
    y = expression(hat(delta)[h])
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "serif") +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: IRF Plots — PART A Inflation MPS Direct Effect (β_h)
#| output: true
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 5

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# IRF — DIRECT EFFECT OF MPS ON INFLATION (β_h)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# β_h captures the direct effect of a monetary policy shock on inflation,
# holding informality constant. This is the "average" transmission effect
# across all levels of informality.
#   β_h < 0 → contractionary MPS reduces inflation (expected)
#   β_h > 0 → contractionary MPS increases inflation ("price puzzle")
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

irf_infl_beta <- lp_all %>%
  filter(variable == "mps_w",
         dep_var_type == "inflation",
         spec == "baseline")

irf_infl_beta$sample <- factor(irf_infl_beta$sample,
                               levels = c("full", "developed", "emerging"),
                               labels = c("Full Sample", "Developed", "Emerging"))

ggplot(irf_infl_beta, aes(x = horizon, y = estimate)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower_95, ymax = ci_upper_95),
              fill = "#2C7FB8", alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower_90, ymax = ci_upper_90),
              fill = "#2C7FB8", alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_line(color = "#2C7FB8", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(color = "#2C7FB8", size = 2.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(estimate, 3)), nudge_y = 0.35, size = 3.5, family = "serif") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  facet_wrap(~ sample, ncol = 3) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:max_horizon) +
  labs(
    title = "Impulse Response: Direct Effect of MPS on Inflation",
    subtitle = expression(paste(beta[h], " (coefficient on MPS) from the baseline LP specification")),
    x = "Horizon (years)",
    y = expression(hat(beta)[h]),
    caption = "Shaded areas: 90% (dark) and 95% (light) confidence intervals. Country-clustered SEs."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "serif") +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30")
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: IRF Plots — PART B Log GDP p.c. MPS Direct Effect (β_h)
#| output: true
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 5

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# IRF — DIRECT EFFECT OF MPS ON LOG GDP PER CAPITA (β_h)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# β_h captures the direct effect of a monetary policy shock on log GDP p.c.
#   β_h < 0 → contractionary MPS reduces output (expected)
#   β_h > 0 → contractionary MPS increases output (unusual)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

irf_lgdp_beta <- lp_all %>%
  filter(variable == "mps_w",
         dep_var_type == "lgdp_pc",
         spec == "baseline")

irf_lgdp_beta$sample <- factor(irf_lgdp_beta$sample,
                               levels = c("full", "developed", "emerging"),
                               labels = c("Full Sample", "Developed", "Emerging"))

ggplot(irf_lgdp_beta, aes(x = horizon, y = estimate)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower_95, ymax = ci_upper_95),
              fill = "#D95F02", alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower_90, ymax = ci_upper_90),
              fill = "#D95F02", alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_line(color = "#D95F02", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(color = "#D95F02", size = 2.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(estimate, 3)), nudge_y = 0.03, size = 3.5, family = "serif") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  facet_wrap(~ sample, ncol = 3) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:max_horizon) +
  labs(
    title = "Impulse Response: Direct Effect of MPS on Log GDP per Capita",
    subtitle = expression(paste(beta[h], " (coefficient on MPS) from the baseline LP specification")),
    x = "Horizon (years)",
    y = expression(hat(beta)[h]),
    caption = "Shaded areas: 90% (dark) and 95% (light) confidence intervals. Country-clustered SEs."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "serif") +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30")
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: IRF Plots — PART C GDP Growth MPS Direct Effect (β_h)
#| output: true
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 5

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# IRF — DIRECT EFFECT OF MPS ON GDP GROWTH (β_h) — SIDELINE
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# β_h captures the direct effect of a monetary policy shock on GDP growth.
#   β_h < 0 → contractionary MPS reduces growth (expected)
#   β_h > 0 → contractionary MPS increases growth (unusual)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

irf_gdpgr_beta <- lp_all %>%
  filter(variable == "mps_w",
         dep_var_type == "gdp_growth",
         spec == "baseline")

irf_gdpgr_beta$sample <- factor(irf_gdpgr_beta$sample,
                                levels = c("full", "developed", "emerging"),
                                labels = c("Full Sample", "Developed", "Emerging"))

ggplot(irf_gdpgr_beta, aes(x = horizon, y = estimate)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower_95, ymax = ci_upper_95),
              fill = "#66A61E", alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower_90, ymax = ci_upper_90),
              fill = "#66A61E", alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_line(color = "#66A61E", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(color = "#66A61E", size = 2.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(estimate, 3)), nudge_y = 0.65, size = 3.5, family = "serif") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  facet_wrap(~ sample, ncol = 3) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:max_horizon) +
  labs(
    title = "Impulse Response: Direct Effect of MPS on GDP Growth (Sideline)",
    subtitle = expression(paste(beta[h], " (coefficient on MPS) from the baseline LP specification")),
    x = "Horizon (years)",
    y = expression(hat(beta)[h]),
    caption = "Shaded areas: 90% (dark) and 95% (light) confidence intervals. Country-clustered SEs."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "serif") +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30")
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: IRF Plots — PART A Inflation MIMIC Direct Effect (γ_h)
#| output: true
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 5

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# IRF — DIRECT EFFECT OF MIMIC ON INFLATION (γ_h)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# γ_h captures the direct association between lagged informality (MIMIC_{t-1})
# and inflation, holding MPS and the interaction constant. This is the "level"
# effect of the informal economy on price dynamics — separate from how it
# moderates monetary transmission (δ_h).
#
#   γ_h > 0 → higher informality is associated with stronger inflation
#   γ_h < 0 → higher informality is associated with weaker inflation
#
# CAVEAT: Diagnostic 3 showed MIMIC is 99.6% between-country. Country FE absorb
# most of that variation, so γ_h is identified off small within-country
# movements and is expected to be imprecise. The interaction δ_h remains the
# core parameter of interest.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

irf_infl_gamma <- lp_all %>%
  filter(variable == "mimic_t_minus1",
         dep_var_type == "inflation",
         spec == "baseline")

irf_infl_gamma$sample <- factor(irf_infl_gamma$sample,
                                levels = c("full", "developed", "emerging"),
                                labels = c("Full Sample", "Developed", "Emerging"))

ggplot(irf_infl_gamma, aes(x = horizon, y = estimate)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower_95, ymax = ci_upper_95),
              fill = "#2C7FB8", alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower_90, ymax = ci_upper_90),
              fill = "#2C7FB8", alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_line(color = "#2C7FB8", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(color = "#2C7FB8", size = 2.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(estimate, 3)), nudge_y = 0.25, size = 3.5, family = "serif") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  facet_wrap(~ sample, ncol = 3) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:max_horizon) +
  labs(
    title = "Impulse Response: Direct Effect of Informality (MIMIC) on Inflation",
    subtitle = expression(paste(gamma[h], " (coefficient on MIMIC) from the baseline LP specification")),
    x = "Horizon (years)",
    y = expression(hat(gamma)[h]),
    caption = "Shaded areas: 90% (dark) and 95% (light) confidence intervals. Country-clustered SEs."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "serif") +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30")
  )

```


```{r}
#| label: IRF Plots — PART B Log GDP p.c. MIMIC Direct Effect (γ_h)
#| output: true
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 5

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# IRF — DIRECT EFFECT OF MIMIC ON LOG GDP PER CAPITA (γ_h)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# γ_h captures the direct association between lagged informality (MIMIC_{t-1})
# and log GDP per capita, holding MPS and the interaction constant.
#
#   γ_h > 0 → higher informality is associated with higher output (unusual)
#   γ_h < 0 → higher informality is associated with lower output
#              (consistent with the "informality as productivity drag" view)
#
# CAVEAT: Same as Part A — MIMIC is 99.6% between-country (Diagnostic 3), so
# country FE absorb most of its variation. γ_h is expected to be imprecise;
# the interaction δ_h is the core parameter.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

irf_lgdp_gamma <- lp_all %>%
  filter(variable == "mimic_t_minus1",
         dep_var_type == "lgdp_pc",
         spec == "baseline")

irf_lgdp_gamma$sample <- factor(irf_lgdp_gamma$sample,
                                levels = c("full", "developed", "emerging"),
                                labels = c("Full Sample", "Developed", "Emerging"))

ggplot(irf_lgdp_gamma, aes(x = horizon, y = estimate)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower_95, ymax = ci_upper_95),
              fill = "#D95F02", alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower_90, ymax = ci_upper_90),
              fill = "#D95F02", alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_line(color = "#D95F02", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(color = "#D95F02", size = 2.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(estimate, 3)), nudge_y = 0.009, size = 3.5, family = "serif") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  facet_wrap(~ sample, ncol = 3) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:max_horizon) +
  labs(
    title = "Impulse Response: Direct Effect of Informality (MIMIC) on Log GDP per Capita",
    subtitle = expression(paste(gamma[h], " (coefficient on MIMIC) from the baseline LP specification")),
    x = "Horizon (years)",
    y = expression(hat(gamma)[h]),
    caption = "Shaded areas: 90% (dark) and 95% (light) confidence intervals. Country-clustered SEs."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "serif") +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30")
  )

```


```{r}
#| label: IRF Plots — PART C GDP Growth MIMIC Direct Effect (γ_h)
#| output: true
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 5

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# IRF — DIRECT EFFECT OF MIMIC ON GDP GROWTH (γ_h) — SIDELINE
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# γ_h captures the direct association between lagged informality (MIMIC_{t-1})
# and the GDP growth rate, holding MPS and the interaction constant.
#
#   γ_h > 0 → higher informality is associated with faster growth
#   γ_h < 0 → higher informality is associated with slower growth
#
# CAVEAT: Same as Parts A and B — γ_h is expected to be imprecise because
# country FE absorb the overwhelmingly between-country variation in MIMIC.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

irf_gdpgr_gamma <- lp_all %>%
  filter(variable == "mimic_t_minus1",
         dep_var_type == "gdp_growth",
         spec == "baseline")

irf_gdpgr_gamma$sample <- factor(irf_gdpgr_gamma$sample,
                                 levels = c("full", "developed", "emerging"),
                                 labels = c("Full Sample", "Developed", "Emerging"))

ggplot(irf_gdpgr_gamma, aes(x = horizon, y = estimate)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower_95, ymax = ci_upper_95),
              fill = "#66A61E", alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower_90, ymax = ci_upper_90),
              fill = "#66A61E", alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_line(color = "#66A61E", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(color = "#66A61E", size = 2.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(estimate, 3)), nudge_y = 0.23, size = 3.5, family = "serif") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  facet_wrap(~ sample, ncol = 3) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:max_horizon) +
  labs(
    title = "Impulse Response: Direct Effect of Informality (MIMIC) on GDP Growth (Sideline)",
    subtitle = expression(paste(gamma[h], " (coefficient on MIMIC) from the baseline LP specification")),
    x = "Horizon (years)",
    y = expression(hat(gamma)[h]),
    caption = "Shaded areas: 90% (dark) and 95% (light) confidence intervals. Country-clustered SEs."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "serif") +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30")
  )

```


```{r}
#| label: IRF Plots — All Coefficients Summary Table (Full Sample)
#| output: true

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SUMMARY TABLE — ALL BASELINE COEFFICIENTS ACROSS HORIZONS
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Full regression output for the baseline spec, not just the interaction.
# Useful for the paper's appendix or results tables.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("  FULL COEFFICIENT TABLE — BASELINE SPECIFICATION (Full Sample)\n")
cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")

cat("\n── Inflation model ──\n")
lp_all %>%
  filter(spec == "baseline", sample == "full", dep_var_type == "inflation") %>%
  select(variable, horizon, estimate, std_error, p_value) %>%
  mutate(estimate = round(estimate, 4),
         std_error = round(std_error, 4),
         p_value = round(p_value, 3),
         sig = case_when(p_value < 0.01 ~ "***",
                         p_value < 0.05 ~ "**",
                         p_value < 0.10 ~ "*",
                         TRUE ~ "")) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  print(row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n── Log GDP p.c. model ──\n")
lp_all %>%
  filter(spec == "baseline", sample == "full", dep_var_type == "lgdp_pc") %>%
  select(variable, horizon, estimate, std_error, p_value) %>%
  mutate(estimate = round(estimate, 4),
         std_error = round(std_error, 4),
         p_value = round(p_value, 3),
         sig = case_when(p_value < 0.01 ~ "***",
                         p_value < 0.05 ~ "**",
                         p_value < 0.10 ~ "*",
                         TRUE ~ "")) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  print(row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n── GDP growth model ──\n")
lp_all %>%
  filter(spec == "baseline", sample == "full", dep_var_type == "gdp_growth") %>%
  select(variable, horizon, estimate, std_error, p_value) %>%
  mutate(estimate = round(estimate, 4),
         std_error = round(std_error, 4),
         p_value = round(p_value, 3),
         sig = case_when(p_value < 0.01 ~ "***",
                         p_value < 0.05 ~ "**",
                         p_value < 0.10 ~ "*",
                         TRUE ~ "")) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  print(row.names = FALSE)

```

```{r}
#| label: IRF Plots — All Coefficients Summary Table (Developed)
#| output: true

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SUMMARY TABLE — ALL BASELINE COEFFICIENTS ACROSS HORIZONS
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Full regression output for the baseline spec, not just the interaction.
# Useful for the paper's appendix or results tables.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("  FULL COEFFICIENT TABLE — BASELINE SPECIFICATION (Developed)\n")
cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")

cat("\n── Inflation model ──\n")
lp_all %>%
  filter(spec == "baseline", sample == "developed", dep_var_type == "inflation") %>%
  select(variable, horizon, estimate, std_error, p_value) %>%
  mutate(estimate = round(estimate, 4),
         std_error = round(std_error, 4),
         p_value = round(p_value, 3),
         sig = case_when(p_value < 0.01 ~ "***",
                         p_value < 0.05 ~ "**",
                         p_value < 0.10 ~ "*",
                         TRUE ~ "")) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  print(row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n── Log GDP p.c. model ──\n")
lp_all %>%
  filter(spec == "baseline", sample == "developed", dep_var_type == "lgdp_pc") %>%
  select(variable, horizon, estimate, std_error, p_value) %>%
  mutate(estimate = round(estimate, 4),
         std_error = round(std_error, 4),
         p_value = round(p_value, 3),
         sig = case_when(p_value < 0.01 ~ "***",
                         p_value < 0.05 ~ "**",
                         p_value < 0.10 ~ "*",
                         TRUE ~ "")) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  print(row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n── GDP growth model ──\n")
lp_all %>%
  filter(spec == "baseline", sample == "developed", dep_var_type == "gdp_growth") %>%
  select(variable, horizon, estimate, std_error, p_value) %>%
  mutate(estimate = round(estimate, 4),
         std_error = round(std_error, 4),
         p_value = round(p_value, 3),
         sig = case_when(p_value < 0.01 ~ "***",
                         p_value < 0.05 ~ "**",
                         p_value < 0.10 ~ "*",
                         TRUE ~ "")) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  print(row.names = FALSE)

```

```{r}
#| label: IRF Plots — All Coefficients Summary Table (Emerging)
#| output: true

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SUMMARY TABLE — ALL BASELINE COEFFICIENTS ACROSS HORIZONS
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Full regression output for the baseline spec, not just the interaction.
# Useful for the paper's appendix or results tables.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("  FULL COEFFICIENT TABLE — BASELINE SPECIFICATION (Emerging)\n")
cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")

cat("\n── Inflation model ──\n")
lp_all %>%
  filter(spec == "baseline", sample == "emerging", dep_var_type == "inflation") %>%
  select(variable, horizon, estimate, std_error, p_value) %>%
  mutate(estimate = round(estimate, 4),
         std_error = round(std_error, 4),
         p_value = round(p_value, 3),
         sig = case_when(p_value < 0.01 ~ "***",
                         p_value < 0.05 ~ "**",
                         p_value < 0.10 ~ "*",
                         TRUE ~ "")) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  print(row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n── Log GDP p.c. model ──\n")
lp_all %>%
  filter(spec == "baseline", sample == "emerging", dep_var_type == "lgdp_pc") %>%
  select(variable, horizon, estimate, std_error, p_value) %>%
  mutate(estimate = round(estimate, 4),
         std_error = round(std_error, 4),
         p_value = round(p_value, 3),
         sig = case_when(p_value < 0.01 ~ "***",
                         p_value < 0.05 ~ "**",
                         p_value < 0.10 ~ "*",
                         TRUE ~ "")) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  print(row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n── GDP growth model ──\n")
lp_all %>%
  filter(spec == "baseline", sample == "emerging", dep_var_type == "gdp_growth") %>%
  select(variable, horizon, estimate, std_error, p_value) %>%
  mutate(estimate = round(estimate, 4),
         std_error = round(std_error, 4),
         p_value = round(p_value, 3),
         sig = case_when(p_value < 0.01 ~ "***",
                         p_value < 0.05 ~ "**",
                         p_value < 0.10 ~ "*",
                         TRUE ~ "")) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  print(row.names = FALSE)

```
